text: Jeff was alone at the school. He startled when Abel appeared.

sequence.speaker: Jeff
text: Oh! Abel. I didn't expect... What are you doing up here?

sequence.speaker: Abel
text: I noticed you weren't at the fish boil. Thought I'd see if everything was okay.

sequence.speaker: Jeff
text: That's... kind of you. I'm fine. Just had some work to catch up on.
Abel glanced past Jeff. The school was empty. No desks arranged for use. No papers. No sign of children.

sequence.speaker: Abel
text: Hey, those strange fish at the boil — some of them looked like deep sea creatures. How do they end up in the bay?
Jeff paused. His eyes moved to the floor before answering.

sequence.speaker: Jeff
text: There's a deep sea trench right off the coast. A sinkhole, really. Unnaturally deep.

sequence.speaker: Abel
text: I've... never heard of anything like that.

sequence.speaker: Jeff
text: Most people haven't. The proximity over the years has led to... special fish. Accustomed to both environs.

sequence.speaker: Abel
text: Do those fish not get the bends?
Jeff said the word 'evolution' the way someone might describe a color they'd never actually seen.

sequence.speaker: Jeff
text: You should get back to the boil. They'll notice you're gone.
